div.p: All offsets in decimal bytes · 64-byte cache lines marked
⚠ Stack-size concern (per-connection, single-threaded M3 / per-thread M4)
Linux default thread stack: 8 MB → safe for pool of 64 threads Compile guard: gcc -Wframe-larger-than=131072
+0x000 name[128] char[128] · 128 bytes Lowercase ASCII, null-terminated e.g. "content-type\0" Cache lines 0 – 1 (2 lines)
+0x080 value[1024] char[1024] · 1024 bytes OWS-stripped, obs-fold-unfolded, null-terminated Cache lines 2 – 17 (16 lines)
+0x0010 path[8192] char[8192] · 8192 bytes Raw URL path (NOT percent-decoded) Cache lines 0 – 127 (128 lines)
+0x2010 version[16] char[16] · 16 bytes "HTTP/1.1" or "HTTP/1.0" Cache line 128
+0x2020 headers[32] http_header_t[32] · 36,864 bytes 32 × 1152 bytes/entry Cache lines 129 – 704 (576 lines) ⚠ Dominant stack cost — 36 KB alone

g.text: ── cache line boundaries every 64 bytes through headers[] ──

div.p: +0xB020 header_count int · 4 bytes Valid range: 0 – 32 Cache line 704
+0xB024 (alignment padding) 4 bytes — aligns next const char * to 8-byte boundary
+0xB028 body const char * · 8 bytes Zero-copy pointer into caller's recv buffer Points to first byte after \r\n\r\n NULL if no body bytes captured Cache line 704
+0xB030 body_len size_t · 8 bytes Bytes from body to end of buffer 0 if body == NULL Cache line 704
+0xB038 end Total: ≈ 45,108 bytes · 705 cache lines sizeof(http_request_t) ≈ 45,108 B
